final_message:
Yay, I love your enthusiasm! Let's imagine a delicious pizza fresh out of the oven. A fraction simply represents a part of a whole thing, just like a slice of that pizza. 

When we write a fraction, the bottom number is called the denominator. It tells you exactly how many equal slices the whole pizza is cut into. The top number is called the numerator, and it tells you how many of those yummy slices you are taking to eat! Just remember, if we ever want to compare two fractions, our whole pizzas must always be the exact same size. 

Does this pizza idea make sense to you? 🍕

orchestrator_intent.goal: Explain the concept of a fraction as a part of a whole using the pizza analogy.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student expressed readiness to learn. The dialogue agent should continue the interrupted explanation of the fraction concept.

current_section_content: A fraction represents a part of a whole. The whole must always be the same size when you are comparing fractions. The bottom number (denominator) tells you how many equal parts the whole is cut into, and the top number (numerator) tells you how many of those parts you are taking.
session_summary: Mithilesh Singh is beginning a new math session focused on fractions. Because the session is currently at the initial greeting and introduction stage, specific areas of strong understanding and struggles have not yet been assessed.
